item.title: Sales Consultant Data & Analytics (w/m/d) – infas 360
item.description: infas 360 infas 360 berät Wirtschaft und Wissenschaft im Nutzen aller verfügbaren Daten und Analysen, um bessere strategische Entscheidungen zu treffen sowie operative Planungen und Maßnahmen zu optimieren. Basis der Beratung sind die Sammlung und Kombination aller verfügbaren Datenquellen, die fortlaufend aktualisiert und analytisch aufbereitet werden. Zudem verfügen wir mit easymap über eine der international führenden Anwendungen für Business Mapping. Die infas 360 hat sich als Teil der international agierenden Ipsos Gruppe als Full-Service-Anbieter darauf spezialisiert soziales Handeln, gesellschaftlich wie ökonomisch, in ihrer räumlichen und zeitlichen Dimension darstellbar zu machen. Das Location Data & Analytics Team der Infas 360, einem Ipsos-Unternehmen, entwickelt standardisierte und integrierte Geo-, Sach- und Markt-Datenprodukte und liefert individuelle Analysen für unsere Kunden. Unternehmen, Institute und Behörden nutzen unsere umfassenden und verlässlichen Datenprodukte für kritische Entscheidungen im Marketing, Vertrieb, Logistik, Bau- und Umweltplanung und für viele weitere Bereiche. Für die Verstärkung unseres Teams suchen wir frühstens ab dem 01.01.2027 an unserem Hauptsitz in Bonn in Vollzeit eine:n Sales Consultant Data & Analytics (w/m/d). Deine Rolle bei uns Du gestaltest die Geschäftsentwicklung in den Bereichen Geomarketing, Customer Analytics und Marketing Research aktiv mit. Du treibst die vertriebliche Weiterentwicklung der Datenprodukte der infas 360 voran, dabei vorrangig die Mikro- und Marktdaten. Du generierst neue Leads. Du führst eigenverantwortlich Kundentermine und -präsentationen durch und unterstützt bei Messe- und Konferenzbesuchen. Du berätst Interessenten und Bestandskunden bei der Anreicherung unternehmenseigener (CRM) Daten durch infas 360 Daten. Du übernimmst die kaufmännische Abwicklung von der Angebotserstellung bis zum Auftragsabschluss. Du konzipierst und führst Analysen durch, wirkst an Angeboten inklusive Kostenkalkulationen mit und präsentierst Ergebnisse – sowohl intern als auch beim Kunden. Was du mitbringst Du hast ein abgeschlossenes Fach- oder Bachelorstudium der (Wirtschafts-) Geografie, Betriebswirtschaftslehre, Soziologie, Marktforschung oder einer themenverwandten Fachrichtung oder bereits erste Berufserfahrung in der Datenanalytik. Du punktest mit Deinem kommunikativen, selbstbewussten und professionellen Auftreten. Du besitzt vertriebliches Geschick, bist offen und hast Freude daran, mit Kunden zu arbeiten. Du zeichnest dich durch einen zielorientierten, selbst organisierten und strukturierten Arbeitsstil aus. Du verfügst über erste Berufserfahrung in Geomarketing, Datenanalytik und Vertrieb erklärungsbedürftiger Dienstleistungen/Consultative Selling. Du verfügst über mittlere bis professionelle Kenntnisse in MS Word, Excel und Outlook, GIS-Kenntnisse sind wünschenswert. Du besitzt verhandlungssichere Deutsch- und sichere Englischkenntnisse; weitere Sprachen sind willkommen. Das bieten wir dir Wir arbeiten in flachen Hierarchien mit kurzen Entscheidungswegen. Wir bieten flexible Arbeitszeiten und Home-Office (60/40). Wir schaffen Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten und die Chance, sich auch in Neuem auszuprobieren. Wir ermöglichen den Bezug eines rabattierten Deutschlandtickets. Wir bieten Betriebliche Altersvorsorge und vermögenswirksame Leistungen. Wir besuchen regelmäßige sportliche, kulturelle oder Fun-Team-Events. Klingt spannend? Dann bewirb dich jetzt! Wir freuen uns auf deine Bewerbungsunterlagen (Lebenslauf, Zeugnisse und optional ein kurzes Anschreiben) unter Angabe deines frühestmöglichen Eintrittstermins und deiner Gehaltsvorstellung.
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Research Executive-Ipsos UU-Beijing China
item.description: Job responsibilities: Assist in preparing project proposals; Independently translate project outlines; Assist in designing project outlines; Organize and translate interview transcripts; Assist in report writing; Other project-related work. Job requirements: Bachelor's degree or above, with a preference for majors in psychology or sociology; Priority given to those with market research internship experience; Familiarity and practical experience with qualitative research methods and project processes; Excellent written and verbal communication skills in both Chinese and English; Excellent interpersonal and communication skills; Proficient computer skills (Office software), familiar with SPSS statistical software; Good time management, project management, problem-solving, and teamwork skills; Outgoing, proactive, creative, and resilient.
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Research Executive
item.description: JOB PROFILE: RESEARCH EXECUTIVE PURPOSE To be an effective team member to the business area and Division, in the design, set up and management of research projects and administrative tasks To learn and master the research basics and undertake project management tasks with supervision ENVIRONMENT Ipsos is among the world’s largest global research companies, with a presence across 220 cities and 90 markets. Our ambition is to make market research one of the primary means to understand contemporary society and economy. And to make Ipsos a strategic partner for those who wish to make better understand the world and play an active part in it, with lasting success. OUTPUTS/ACCOUNTABILITY Oversees the entire research process from start to finish together with the Senior Research Manager Key tasks includes: Timeline planning, desk research, screener design, questionnaire design, sampling, analysis plans, tab specs, data processing, report design / templates, data analysis and insights and presentations Ensures research basics and disciplines are implemented and upheld, including: logic checks, checklists, document standards, policies and procedures Responsible for overall quality control and all quality control documentation Ensures the process runs according to deadlines and budget together with the Project Manager Attempts initial value add / insights at each stage (development area) Develops basic Solutions knowledge Ensures integration of information from multiple sources where applicable and available Conducts desk research to add value to all reports / presentations and assist the client in understanding the South African market Assists with research design for entire project / process on small pitches Experience Knowledge of basic statistics Experience in questionnaire design, table specifications and project management Understanding of basic methodologies and techniques to provide input into research design Previous experience as a project manager or a research executive in a market research company will be an advantage Experience in innovation research or customised quantitative research will be an advantage Additional requirements: Excellent command of spoken and written English Exceptional presentation skills Excellent leadership and project management skills Must be able to build and maintain internal and external client relationships Analytical skills / Training skills / Delegation skills Excellent computing skills especially in Microsoft Word, Excel and PowerPoint Proactive with high energy levels Qualifications Degree in Business, Social Science, Statistics or related studies Note: This is a role that will report to a manager based in another country. While there are no time zone differences, you should be comfortable collaborating across borders. Strong communication skills, proactive updates, and adaptability are essential to ensure seamless teamwork in a international setup.
item.dc:creator: Ipsos
item.polintel:location: Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

item.title: F2F Intern
item.description: In Ipsos Doxa siamo appassionati di persone, mercati, brand e società. La nostra ambizione è diventare partner di tutti i clienti che vogliono comprendere in profondità e dettaglio la loro audience, i mercati, i consumatori, e le marche in un mondo in rapida e continua evoluzione. Siamo un solido brand presente in 90 paesi e più di 20.000 professionisti, con un posizionamento unico di esperienze specifiche che allo stesso tempo si integrano in team dedicati. Crediamo che i nostri clienti ci chiedano molto di più di semplici dati. Hanno bisogno di un partner che lavori con loro per produrre informazioni puntuali e pertinenti e trasformarle in concrete strategie di business. Questo è il motivo per cui i nostri esperti mettono al servizio di aziende e istituzioni tutta la propria competenza e capacità di ascolto, non solo per fornire le rilevazioni più accurate ma anche per adattarle alle specifiche esigenze. Ci poniamo un unico obiettivo: quello di offrire a tutti i nostri clienti una comprensione totale di mercati, società e persone. Per fare ciò, usiamo un mix unico di competenze professionali, tecnologia e know-how tecnico applicando ad ogni cosa che facciamo i principi di sicurezza, semplicità, velocità e concretezza. In definitiva, crediamo che alla base del successo vi sia una semplice verità: YOU ACT BETTER WHEN YOU ARE SURE! La posizione Per la nostra sede di Milano siamo alla ricerca di una persona di talento da inserire in un percorso di stage nel ruolo di F2F INTERN Cosa farai? Supporterai il Fieldwork Manager e il Team Operations nelle diverse fasi operative del fieldwork per ricerche di mercato quantitative; Avrai contatto gli intervistatori e darai supporto nella gestione della rete di rilevatori e intervistatori sul territorio (individuazione nuovi intervistatori); Preparerai materiali, organizzazione e supporto nei briefing ai rilevatori e intervistatori su come condurre le interviste (ad es. assegnazione delle interviste e simulazioni, metodologie ad hoc da seguire, definizione dei contenuti dei questionari); Monitorerai l’avanzamento delle interviste su base quantitativa e/o secondo indicatori di performance; Supporterai il Fieldwork Manager nell’utilizzo di software gestionali specifici (inclusa la piattaforma Ipsos I-field) e interfacciandoti con varie figure interne (Data Processing, Market Researchers, ecc.); Acquisirai competenze di base nell’esecuzione degli aspetti operativi dei progetti di ricerca quantitativa; Chi cerchiamo? Persone curiose e appassionate, con: laurea in discipline economiche o umanistiche ottima conoscenza del pacchetto Office (in particolare Excel) Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese Forte predisposizione alla precisione, alla cura dei dettagli e al lavoro di squadra Orientamento agli obiettivi definiti Cosa troverai un percorso altamente formativo all’interno di un Gruppo internazionale; un onboarding guidato e strutturato da Line Manager e HRBP per facilitare il tuo ingresso e inserimento in azienda; un ambiente di lavoro stimolante e molto attento alle persone e alle politiche in termini di ESG: Ambiente, Sostenibilità, People - D&I ampia flessibilità di orario e di lavoro da remoto per gestire al meglio il tuo tempo partecipazione ad attività interne di Benessere aziendale; formazione continua on the job, accesso alla piattaforma e-learning (Ipsos Training Center); un Team People & Culture HR a tua disposizione con cui potrai sempre confrontarti in caso di bisogno; l'opportunità di lavorare a stretto contatto con persone esperte del settore Altre informazioni Sede di Milano Rimborso spese di 700 euro + ticket restaurant Inizio stage: giugno 2026 6 mesi, 38 ore settimanali Taking Responsibility Ipsos riconosce la necessità di costruire una cultura inclusiva che valorizzi l'individualità e le diverse prospettive di ciascun lavoratore e lavoratrice. Da oltre 40 anni, la nostra missione è generare e analizzare dati sulla società, sui mercati, sui marchi e sui comportamenti per fornire ai nostri clienti gli approfondimenti che migliorano la loro comprensione del mondo. Questo non potrebbe essere realizzato senza le persone di Ipsos che compilano e analizzano questi dati: essi sono l'essenza di ciò che siamo e di ciò che facciamo. Ci impegniamo a offrire pari opportunità, a creare un ambiente che promuova l'inclusione. Ipsos incoraggia il proprio personale ad agire in modo rispettoso e responsabile, in linea con il codice e la nostra policy in materia di diversità e inclusione, diritti umani, uguaglianza e civiltà. Il processo di selezione Una volta ricevuta la tua candidatura, ci prenderemo il tempo necessario per valutarla attentamente. Nel caso in cui il tuo profilo sia in linea per questa o altre posizioni presso l'azienda, ci metteremo in contatto con te. Tuttavia, nel caso in cui non ci sia una corrispondenza al momento, non preoccuparti! ci assicureremo di tenere in considerazione il tuo cv per future opportunità in linea con il tuo profilo e nel totale rispetto della normativa privacy e GDPR. Tutte le candidature saranno prese in considerazione per la posizione senza riguardo all'età, alla razza, al colore della pelle, alla religione, al sesso, all'orientamento sessuale, all'identità di genere, all'origine nazionale, allo status di rifugiato o a qualsiasi altra categoria protetta e non saranno discriminati sulla base della disabilità. I nostri valori Siamo "Proud to be Ipsos" perché crediamo fortemente nella nostra cultura aziendale, incarnando i valori di Integrità – Collaborazione – Curiosità – Spirito Imprenditoriale – Attenzione al Cliente nei rapporti con i colleghi, con i clienti e con la società. Se ti riconosci nella nostra realtà e nei nostri valori, se hai l’ambizione di entrare a far parte di un’azienda dinamica e internazionale e desideri crescere insieme a noi, candidati ora e inizia il tuo viaggio nel mondo Ipsos! I dati personali raccolti ai fini del processo di selezione saranno trattati nel rispetto del Regolamento UE n. 679/2016.
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Research Executive-Ipsos UU-Guangzhou China
item.description: Job responsibilities: Assist in preparing project proposals; Independently translate project outlines; Assist in designing project outlines; Organize and translate interview transcripts; Assist in report writing; Other project-related work. Job requirements: Bachelor's degree or above, with a preference for majors in psychology or sociology; Priority given to those with market research internship experience; Familiarity and practical experience with qualitative research methods and project processes; Excellent written and verbal communication skills in both Chinese and English; Excellent interpersonal and communication skills; Proficient computer skills (Office software), familiar with SPSS statistical software; Good time management, project management, problem-solving, and teamwork skills; Outgoing, proactive, creative, and resilient.
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Data Analyst - Market Research Audience Measurement
item.description: Data Analyst - Audience Measurement Make your mark at Ipsos Ipsos’s Audience measurement team is recruiting a Data Analyst. If you are a self-starter who loves to dive deep into data, solve complex problems, and see your analysis make a real-world impact, this is the perfect opportunity for you. You will be empowered to manage your own tasks and explore data, all while being a vital part of a collaborative and forward-thinking team. This role sits in Ipsos’s Audience Measurement team that uses a deep understanding of people to make sense of audiences and how they consume media. We use these insights to influence media strategy, helping clients to answer crucial questions, such as how to target audiences, maximise attention across platforms, enhance audience experience, and demonstrate or increase audience value. What is in it for you? This is a fantastic opportunity to work on our flagship digital audience measurement tool. It is a unique opportunity that empowers to work autonomously. You will be working on UKOM, the UK body for online audience measurement, which appointed Ipsos as the new supplier of the UK industry standard for online audience measurement. The new approach, Ipsos iris , has a single-source high quality nationally representative panel at its heart and will provide the industry with an unrivalled understanding of online behaviour across PC, smartphone and tablet. The role We are looking for a curious and proactive Data Analyst to join the Ipsos iris team, the industry standard for online audience measurement in the UK. In this role, you are expected to turn our rich, large-scale data into actionable insights that drive business decisions. You will be a key player in understanding our user’s online behaviour, uncover trends, patterns, and stories that will provide value for our clients. On a day to day basis you will be: Using your Python and SQL coding skills Conducting custom data analysis, pulling the data from Big query and Google Cloud. Analysing data collected from different measurement resources Understanding the data workflow and the sources of data Carrying out data analysis, interpreting trends and creating compelling story telling (using the data) Generating insights and reports generation Daily and monthly validation within the deadline Coordinate between the teams – especially between commercial and data science teams to prevent any delays in providing data and communication to the clients About you: Essential Criteria: A Background and similar experience in a market research agency. Solid experience in data analysis, with a strong focus on data quality investigations and issue resolution. Skilled in deep-dive user behaviour analysis, including funnel analysis, cohort studies, user segmentation, and demographic analysis to uncover actionable insights. High proficiency in leveraging SQL (BigQuery) and Python to explore, clean, and analyse very large datasets. Practical experience using AI-powered tools for research, data analysis, and automation to enhance efficiency and depth of insight. Strong Excel skills for data manipulation and ad-hoc analysis. Proven ability to translate complex data problems and analytical results into clear, actionable recommendations for internal stakeholders. Experience creating compelling data visualisations and reports to communicate findings effectively. A proactive and autonomous mindset, with the ability to identify and pursue impactful analytical questions without direct supervision. Excellent teamwork skills combined with the self-starting discipline to manage your own priorities and tasks. Knowledge of APIs and data collection/market research principles. Benefits: We offer a comprehensive benefits package designed to support you as an individual. Our standard benefits include 25 days annual leave, pension contribution, income protection and life assurance. In addition, there are a range of health & wellbeing, financial benefits and professional development opportunities. We realise you may have commitments outside of work and will consider flexible working applications - please highlight what you are looking for when you make your application. We have a hybrid approach to work and ask people to be in the office or with clients for 3 days per week. We are committed to equality, treating people fairly, promoting a positive and inclusive working environment and ensuring we have diversity of people and views. We recognise that this is important for our business success - a more diverse workforce will enable us to better reflect and understand the world we research and ultimately deliver better research and insight to our clients. We are proud to be a member of the Disability Confident scheme, certified as Level 1 Disability Confident Committed. We are dedicated to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment process.
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Research Executive-Ipsos UU-Shanghai China
item.description: Job responsibilities: Assist in preparing project proposals; Independently translate project outlines; Assist in designing project outlines; Organize and translate interview transcripts; Assist in report writing; Other project-related work. Job requirements: Bachelor's degree or above, with a preference for majors in psychology or sociology; Priority given to those with market research internship experience; Familiarity and practical experience with qualitative research methods and project processes; Excellent written and verbal communication skills in both Chinese and English; Excellent interpersonal and communication skills; Proficient computer skills (Office software), familiar with SPSS statistical software; Good time management, project management, problem-solving, and teamwork skills; Outgoing, proactive, creative, and resilient.
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Research Analyst - Innovation
item.description: Why this role is important at Ipsos: As a Research Analyst , you play a role in supporting the delivery of strategic insights to our clients through the execution of research projects. Your work includes a variety or studies requiring sharp time management skills and a keen attention to detail. This is a position where you will learn to be a major force in quantitative research design and gain experience managing key market research projects. What you can expect to be doing: Works with project team and appropriate internal resources to meet client needs. Participates in client communication (email, phone, etc.) as appropriate to gain understanding of needs and to capture client requests and feedback to incorporate in project documents Familiarizes self with key clients/accounts within the Service Line Supports the preparation of reports and presentations May share files and updates with clients Works alongside project lead in the execution and delivery of reliable and timely data to our clients by owning tasks throughout the project lifecycle such as link checking, data quality checks and report population. Focuses on executional excellence with increased independence Understands the project lifecycle for custom research and manages projects from client approval to deliverables with project lead oversight Collaborates with internal Ops partners and project lead in project execution Manages project timelines Mentors new Analysts on core competencies Questionnaire review and revision Creating and submitting project materials Managing internal project partners Reviewing and finalizing links Monitoring fieldwork Reviewing and finalizing data tables Spec'ing, charting, and proofing report slides Managing multiple projects/priorities and working towards resolving problems that arise. Participates in mentorship of new Analysts and Interns. This may include shadowing on projects and providing guidance on tasks throughout the project lifecycle Manages internal project partners with increased independence, in order to help keep the project/tasks on schedule and within scope Participates in analytic plan meetings with the project team. Creates report shell with project team direction and is responsible for timely and accurate report creation Reviews survey data and starts to analyze and interpret survey results (may assist with writing headlines) This might be the job for you if you have: Bachelor’s degree or Master's in Market Research 1+ years of primary, quantitative research experience in a professional setting (including internship experience) Strong Word, Excel, PowerPoint skills Outstanding attention to detail, accuracy, and thoroughness in completing all tasks Highly organized/ Good time management/ Ability to effectively manage multiple projects and tasks Clear, concise written communication skills Ability to positively interact with internal support teams Ability to easily understand and adapt to in-house operational forms, process, and software If you don’t meet 100% of the requirements, we encourage all who feel they might be a fit for the opportunity to apply. We may consider a variety of backgrounds for a particular role and are also committed to considering candidates for available positions throughout our organization, not just the one you’re applying to! In accordance with NY/CO/WA law, the estimated base salary for this role is $59,000 to $72,000. Your final base salary will be determined based on several non-discriminatory factors which may include but are not limited to location, work experience, skills, knowledge, education and/or certifications. What’s in it for you: At Ipsos you’ll experience opportunities for Career Development, an exceptional benefits package (including generous PTO, healthcare plans, wellness benefits), a flexible workplace policy, and a strong collaborative culture. To find out more about all the great reasons to work at Ipsos, how we’re making an impact around the world, and more about our benefits and employee programs, please visit: Why Work at Ipsos | US Commitment to Diversity Ipsos recognizes the necessity of building an inclusive culture that values each employee’s individuality and diverse perspectives. For more than 40 years, our mission has been to generate and analyze data about society, markets, brands, and behaviors to provide our clients with the insights that elevate their understanding of the world. This could not be fulfilled without Ipsos’ diverse employees who compile and analyze this data—they are the essence of who we are and what we do. We are committed to providing equal opportunity to all employees, creating an environment that promotes inclusion, and enabling employees from all walks of life to flourish. Ipsos encourages our employees to act in a respectful and responsible manner, in line with code of best practices concerning diversity and inclusion, human rights, equality, and civility for every individual. Ipsos is An Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, or any other protected class and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability. #LI-FM1 #LI-HYBRID
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Statistician
item.description: This Associate Statistician (Data Science Focus) will be a member of the USPA Data Analytics and Applied Sciences (DAAS) group. DAAS is a team of survey methodologists, statisticians, and Ph.D. social and behavioral scientists located in Ipsos offices throughout the US. DAAS researchers work across disciplines to develop custom, high quality data and analytic solutions, and produce empirical insights used throughout the USPA practice. DAAS researchers are passionate about research, data, problem-solving, and innovation. Key Responsibilities Support diverse USPA project teams in addressing client research needs via developing efficient and robust study designs, and executing comprehensive, advanced inferential and predictive analyses. Assist senior researchers in generating complex weighting adjustments using machine learning as well as classical statistical procedures, implementing variance adjustments for survey inferences, blending samples, and monitoring data quality. Prepare internal and external data for analysis, and review data as they come in from the field. Serve as supporting author on client technical deliverables, as well as narrative proposals. Actively contribute to technical literature reviews and solutioning sessions in the domains of statistics, data science, causal inference, synthetic data, and data integration. Work as part of interdisciplinary project teams to interpret and cross-validate study findings, develop visualizations and insights, and communicate results to clients and stakeholders. Interface with other technical teams across Ipsos to facilitate effective deployment of high performance computing resources. The Ideal Candidate has the following Skills, Experience, and Attributes Masters or PhD in statistics, biostatistics, data science, computer science, or a relevant highly-quantitative social or behavioral science field. Between 2-6 years of experience (depending on credential) in an applied research settings, preferably serving Federal or state statistical or policy agencies in the domains of population and / or behavioral research. Experience with Bayesian modeling and / or supporting the design and implementation of complex sampling and weighting plans a plus. Formal training in one or more forms of longitudinal data analysis and their application to social and behavioral research (i.e., growth curve modeling, mixed effects models, panel methods, failure time / event history analysis, time series, etc.). Experience with Census, health, public opinion, and demographic data. Advanced skills in R and Python (must have experience using PyTorch); skill in SAS preferred. Ability to convey complex technical topics to non-technical audiences, both verbally and in written form. Proactive, and comfortable working within tight and shifting timelines. Ability to support multiple projects simultaneously, while maintaining a strong client service orientation. Unflagging attention to detail and commitment to the accuracy and completeness of information, quality of results, and robustness of findings. Team player with strong interpersonal skills, and the potential to grow as a multidisciplinary quantitative researcher in a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization. Flexible and comprehensive thinker with enthusiasm for learning and population research; solid foundation in the theoretical and practical aspects of applying statistics as well as data science tools and principles to custom, hypothesis-driven social and behavioral research. In order to comply with US Federal Government requirements, the successful candidate must be a US Citizen. If you don’t meet 100% of the requirements, we encourage all who feel they might be a fit for the opportunity to apply. We may consider a variety of backgrounds for a particular role and are also committed to considering candidates for available positions throughout our organization, not just the one you’re applying to! In accordance with NY/CO/CA/WA law, the estimated base salary range for this role is $100,000 to $120,000. Your final base salary will be determined based on several non-discriminatory factors which may include but are not limited to location, work experience, skills, knowledge, education and/or certifications. What’s in it for you: At Ipsos you’ll experience opportunities for Career Development, an exceptional benefits package (including generous PTO, healthcare plans, wellness benefits), a flexible workplace policy, and a strong collaborative culture. To find out more about all the great reasons to work at Ipsos, how we’re making an impact around the world, and more about our benefits and employee programs, please visit: Why Work at Ipsos | US Why Work at Ipsos | CA Commitment to Diversity Ipsos recognizes the necessity of building an inclusive culture that values each employee’s individuality and diverse perspectives. For more than 40 years, our mission has been to generate and analyze data about society, markets, brands, and behaviors to provide our clients with the insights that elevate their understanding of the world. This could not be fulfilled without Ipsos’ diverse employees who compile and analyze this data—they are the essence of who we are and what we do. We are committed to providing equal opportunity to all employees, creating an environment that promotes inclusion, and enabling employees from all walks of life to flourish. Ipsos encourages our employees to act in a respectful and responsible manner, in line with code of best practices concerning diversity and inclusion, human rights, equality, and civility for every individual. Ipsos is An Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, or any other protected class and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability. #LI-TL1 #LI-Hybrid
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Data Analyst, Global Modeling Unit
item.description: What you can expect to be doing: Clean, structure, code, and summarize survey and secondary data Use statistical software (SPSS, R, etc.) and business software (Excel) to uncover and evaluate solutions, and assist project staff in running statistical analyses Apply programming skills (Excel VBA, Python, etc.) to help automate standard statistical procedures and chart creation. Demonstrate your understanding of multivariate analysis and Ipsos proprietary products, and work with Client Service from understanding business questions, to design, analysis and application Work from an analytic plan, assemble Excel outputs or PowerPoint decks to provide results and explanations for Client Service Consistently ensure the timely delivery of error free work This might be the job for you if you have: BS degree or MS degree in Quantitative Marketing or Social Sciences, Statistics, Applied Mathematics, Marketing Research, or a related field of study with an analytic/quantitative/research focus Statistical analysis background is desirable Knowledge and hands-on experience of SPSS or other statistical analysis packages Python, R, VBA coding experience a bonus Understanding of text analytics and AI in marketing research a plus Good communication skills and ability to present data findings in an easy to understand manner to internal and external clients less familiar with statistical techniques Inquisitive personality with a strong desire to learn Ability to work both independently and as part of a team Good attention to details and logical decision-making process If you don’t meet 100% of the requirements, we encourage all who feel they might be a fit for the opportunity to apply. We may consider a variety of backgrounds for a particular role and are also committed to considering candidates for available positions throughout our organization, not just the one you’re applying to! In accordance with NY/CO/CA/WA law, the estimated base salary range for this role is $59.000 to $71,000. Your final base salary will be determined based on several non-discriminatory factors which may include but are not limited to location, work experience, skills, knowledge, education and/or certifications. What’s in it for you: At Ipsos you’ll experience opportunities for Career Development, an exceptional benefits package (including generous PTO, healthcare plans, wellness benefits), a flexible workplace policy, and a strong collaborative culture. To find out more about all the great reasons to work at Ipsos, how we’re making an impact around the world, and more about our benefits and employee programs, please visit: Why Work at Ipsos | US Commitment to Diversity Ipsos recognizes the necessity of building an inclusive culture that values each employee’s individuality and diverse perspectives. For more than 40 years, our mission has been to generate and analyze data about society, markets, brands, and behaviors to provide our clients with the insights that elevate their understanding of the world. This could not be fulfilled without Ipsos’ diverse employees who compile and analyze this data—they are the essence of who we are and what we do. We are committed to providing equal opportunity to all employees, creating an environment that promotes inclusion, and enabling employees from all walks of life to flourish. Ipsos encourages our employees to act in a respectful and responsible manner, in line with code of best practices concerning diversity and inclusion, human rights, equality, and civility for every individual. Ipsos is An Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, or any other protected class and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability. #LI-FM #LI-Hybrid
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Syndicated Research Analyst, Financial Services
item.description: What makes this role important at Ipsos? We are looking for someone who is passionate about market research and motivated by a fast-paced and challenging work environment. This is an excellent entry-level role for someone who is interested in starting a career in the consulting and insights industry. In this role, you will be responsible for servicing Financial Services Clients (Banks, Credit Unions, Credit Card Companies, etc.) leveraging some of the largest syndicated banking behaviour tracking studies in Canada. As a Research Analyst, you will support the Account Lead and senior team members as they work with these clients to understand their business issues and execute customized research projects to meet their needs. You will support projects from start to finish, including designing analytical plans with senior team members, extracting data through different software, analyzing said data to generate insights, assisting in developing recommendations, and creating compelling stories and presentations that help illuminate the human behaviors behind the data. You will also be responsible for ensuring the studies run smoothly from an operational standpoint. The Research Analyst will report to the Director and join a team of curious-minded researchers and client partners. What you can expect to be doing: Understand and address business needs/issues of respective clients. Manage multiple survey-based market research projects at once, within timeline, including working closely with other team members and colleagues Design plan analyses. Analyze data and draw conclusions; prepare reports and presentations; make recommendations based on the insights that address the client’s business needs. Quality check study questionnaires & perform in-depth quality checks at every stage of the process Liaising with our internal data collection teams, coding & data processing teams Perform data verification Identify and solve project issues as they arise This might be the job for you if you have: Bachelor’s Degree Project management and/or market research experience an asset Outstanding verbal and written communication skills Excellent problem solving, critical thinking and analytical skills Must be self-motivated and entrepreneurial (i.e., ability to think independently). Intellectually curious with a desire to better understand the “why” behind the data. Works well in a team environment with senior and junior colleagues. Proactive, “can do” attitude High attention to detail: ability to balance overall project goal with key milestones and tasks Advanced proficiency with MS Office Suite; specifically, PowerPoint and Excel Self-starter with superior organization skills who thrives in a deadline driven environment Ability to juggle multiple projects and diplomatically respond to conflicting needs Bilingual an asset,but not required. If you don’t meet 100% of the requirements, we encourage all who feel they might be a fit for the opportunity to apply. We may consider a variety of backgrounds for a particular role and are also committed to considering candidates for available positions throughout our organization, not just the one you’re applying to! What’s in it for you: At Ipsos you’ll experience opportunities for Career Development, an exceptional benefits package (including generous PTO, healthcare plans, wellness benefits), a flexible workplace policy, and a strong collaborative culture. To find out more about all the great reasons to work at Ipsos, how we’re making an impact around the world, and more about our benefits and employee programs, please visit: Why Work at Ipsos | CA Commitment to Diversity Ipsos recognizes the necessity of building an inclusive culture that values each employee’s individuality and diverse perspectives. For more than 40 years, our mission has been to generate and analyze data about society, markets, brands, and behaviors to provide our clients with the insights that elevate their understanding of the world. This could not be fulfilled without Ipsos’ diverse employees who compile and analyze this data—they are the essence of who we are and what we do. We are committed to providing equal opportunity to all employees, creating an environment that promotes inclusion, and enabling employees from all walks of life to flourish. Ipsos encourages our employees to act in a respectful and responsible manner, in line with code of best practices concerning diversity and inclusion, human rights, equality, and civility for every individual. Ipsos is An Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, or any other protected class and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability. #LI-KE1#LI-Hybrid #IpsosCanada
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Cloud Platform / Production Engineer
item.description: Senior Platform & Reliability Production Engineer – Audience Measurement Make Your Mark at Ipsos Ipsos CrossMedia is at a pivotal and exciting stage of growth, and we are looking for a motivated Production Engineer to join our innovative Data Science and Engineering teams. This is a fantastic opportunity to work on both high-profile, established projects for blue-chip clients and to contribute to brand-new, greenfield initiatives. What is in it for you? This is a fantastic opportunity for someone to enhance their career and develop key skills. You will get the opportunity to: Build and Manage Cloud Infrastructure: You will own the management of our production and development infrastructure on Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and AWS. This includes our Kubernetes clusters, cloud storage, and identity and access management. Master Infrastructure as Code (IaC): You will design, write, and deploy code to manage our infrastructure using modern IaC tools like Terraform or Pulumi. This is a chance to build your skills in creating repeatable and robust systems. Automate Everything: You will be responsible for our CI/CD pipelines, using tools like GitHub Actions, or Azure DevOps to automate the building, testing, and deployment of our applications and data workflows. Orchestrate Data Workflows: A core part of your role will be creating, maintaining, and monitoring our data ingestion and delivery pipelines using Argo Workflows. You'll ensure that data flows seamlessly and reliably to our clients and internal teams. Ensure System Reliability (SRE Principles): You will monitor the health and performance of our systems, ensuring efficient resource usage. When issues arise, you'll be on the front line, performing root cause analysis, deploying fixes, and providing Level 2 technical support. You'll also contribute to a blameless post-mortem culture to learn from incidents. Drive Continuous Improvement: You will be empowered to propose and implement structural improvements to our platform, from configuration management to system architecture. We want you to help us get better every day. Collaborate Across Teams: You will work closely with our talented Data Scientists, Software Engineers, and Product Owners to build scalable, reliable, and secure solutions that meet their needs. Shared On-Call Responsibility: You will participate in a shared, rota-based, out-of-hours emergency on-call schedule. We strive to build resilient systems to keep this quiet, and we ensure our on-call engineers are well-supported. The Role: As a key member of our team, you will be at the heart of our data platform, ensuring its reliability, scalability, and efficiency. We are constantly evolving our workflows and are committed to investing in cutting-edge technology. If you are passionate about designing, building, and deploying resilient, distributed systems and want to make a tangible impact, you will thrive here. You won't just be maintaining systems; you'll have the autonomy to propose and implement new ideas and technologies that will shape the future of our data infrastructure. About you: The successful candidate will bring: Cloud Platform Experience: Professional experience with a major cloud provider (GCP, AWS, or Azure). We primarily use GCP and AWS, so experience there is a significant plus. Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Knowledge of IaC principles and tools such as Terraform, Pulumi, or Ansible. CI/CD: Knowledge of continuous integration and continuous delivery workflows. Databases: Working knowledge of SQL and experience with relational databases or data warehouses. Scripting and Automation: Proficiency in a scripting language (e.g., Python, Bash) and comfort with the Unix/Linux command line (we expect you to know your way around `grep`, `awk`, `sed`, etc.). ETL/Data Pipelines: Familiarity with data workflows and tools like Argo Workflows. Desirable Skills (Nice to Have) Containerization and Orchestration: Experience with Docker and Kubernetes is highly desirable. Version Control: Proficiency with Git and collaborative workflows (e.g., pull requests, code reviews). Experience of Windows IIS NoSQL Databases: Familiarity with NoSQL databases like MongoDB or DynamoDB. Agile and Project Tracking: Experience with tools like Jira or Azure DevOps. Networking Concepts: A good understanding of networking principles (TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP). Security Best Practices: A good awareness of and interest in security and privacy in the cloud. Benefits: We offer a comprehensive benefits package designed to support you as an individual. Our standard benefits include 25 days annual leave, pension contribution, income protection and life assurance. In addition, there are a range of health & wellbeing, financial benefits and professional development opportunities. We realise you may have commitments outside of work and will consider flexible working applications - please highlight what you are looking for when you make your application. We have a hybrid approach to work and ask people to be in the office or with clients for 3 days per week. We are committed to equality, treating people fairly, promoting a positive and inclusive working environment and ensuring we have diversity of people and views. We recognise that this is important for our business success - a more diverse workforce will enable us to better reflect and understand the world we research and ultimately deliver better research and insight to our clients. We are proud to be a member of the Disability Confident scheme, certified as Level 2 Disability Confident Employer. We are dedicated to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment process. Your application will be reviewed by someone from our Talent Team who will be in touch either way to let you know the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Short term internship, urgent - Root office
item.description: In Switzerland Ipsos is the largest market research company; we are a team of 126, from more than 25 countries and based in Geneva, Basel and Root. To support various our team in Root (Lucerne), we are looking for a Research Executive Intern (for a 3 months term). A written tripartite agreement is mandatory between the intern, the school and Ipsos Suisse. Internship should ideally start asap. Prior to applying, candidates should check their eligibility to a Swiss work permit.
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Contract, Corporate Counsel
item.description: What makes this role important at Ipsos? We are seeking a Contract Corporate Counsel for a 3-month contract to address a wide variety of contractual matters for our U.S. and Canadian clients working with the North America legal team. You will also assist the Group General Counsel with legal matters relating to our global key accounts among other matters as needed. Location: This is a hybrid role with the expectation of being in our Manhattan office 2-3 days per week and will report directly to the Group General Counsel. What you can expect to be doing: Review, draft and negotiate client and vendor contracts, RFPs, NDAs, DPAs, consulting agreements and any other commercial agreement relating to the company’s provision of services. Work closely with client service teams to resolve legal issues related to commercial relationships between Ipsos and its clients Provide advice and support to the business teams on all matters relating to data privacy and security including: personal data of users, consumers, employees and third -parties. data privacy requirements for websites, social media, apps, connected products, e-commerce, customer relationship management and the business teams’ various products and services all laws relating to data privacy and security, in the various jurisdictions in which the business teams operate including the U.S. (e.g., HIPAA, CCPA/CPRA, GLBA, CAN-SPAM, COPPA), Canada (e.g., PIPEDA, CASL), and the EU (the GDPR and the ePrivacy Directive.) Create and improve our standard forms, processes and policies Lead trainings of internal business teams on contractual terms, compliance with relevant corporate policies, applicable laws, and industry codes of conduct Assist with a variety of other day-to-day legal matters as needed Collaborate with Group legal department on Americas-wide and global projects This might be the job for you if you have: Bachelor’s Degree and J.D. degree from accredited institutions required A minimum of 8 years’ experience working on commercial contracts in a law firm or mix of law firm and in-house roles Excellent written and oral communication skills Strong negotiation abilities Excellent time management and organizational skills with the ability to work efficiently and productively Ability to willingly accept coaching and feedback as you develop in the role Ability to work effectively with management team and outside legal, financial and commercial advisors Ability to quickly learn to identify, comprehend and assess the legal issues that are specific to the market research industry Interact with local and regional leadership with a positive, approachable attitude, and with the capability to appropriately push back when required Passion for the job and for protecting Ipsos A high level of flexibility and initiative Sense of ownership of responsibilities Ability to remain calm and focused in high-pressure situations Excellent interpersonal skills, which are necessary for effective development and maintenance of internal and external client relationships Ability to maintain confidentiality and assist on sensitive matters with discretion Nice to have: CIPP/E and CIPP/US certification Experience and/or knowledge of intellectual property (copyrights, patents and trademarks) and promotions (advertising pre-publication review, contests and sweepstakes) Mergers and acquisitions experience Data privacy experience Experience with HR or real estate matters If you don’t meet 100% of the requirements, we encourage all who feel they might be a fit for the opportunity to apply. We may consider a variety of backgrounds for a particular role and are also committed to considering candidates for available positions throughout our organization, not just the one you’re applying to! In accordance with NY/CO/CA/WA law, the estimated base salary range for this role is $230,000 to $250,000. Your final base salary will be determined based on several non-discriminatory factors which may include but are not limited to location, work experience, skills, knowledge, education and/or certifications. What’s in it for you: At Ipsos you’ll experience opportunities for Career Development, an exceptional benefits package (including generous PTO, healthcare plans, wellness benefits), a flexible workplace policy, and a strong collaborative culture. To find out more about all the great reasons to work at Ipsos, how we’re making an impact around the world, and more about our benefits and employee programs, please visit : Why Work at Ipsos | US Inclusive Workplace Commitm ent: Ipsos recognizes the necessity of building an inclusive culture that values each employee’s individuality and diverse perspectives. For more than 40 years, our mission has been to generate and analyze data about society, markets, brands, and behaviors to provide our clients with the insights that elevate their understanding of the world. This could not be fulfilled without Ipsos’ diverse employees who compile and analyze this data—they are the essence of who we are and what we do. We are committed to providing equal opportunity to all employees, creating an environment that promotes inclusion, and enabling employees from all walks of life to flourish. Ipsos encourages our employees to act in a respectful and responsible manner, in line with code of best practices concerning diversity and inclusion, human rights, equality, and civility for every individual. Ipsos is An Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, or any other protected class and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability. #LI-AE1 #HYBRID
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Quality Analyst
item.description: Title: Quality Analyst Location: Parsippany NJ Who We Are: Ipsos is one of the world’s largest research companies and currently the only one primarily managed by researchers, ranking as a #1 full-service research organization for four consecutive years. With over 75 different data-driven solutions, and presence in 90 markets, Ipsos brings together research, implementation, methodological, and subject-matter experts from around the world, combining thematic and technical experts to deliver top-quality research and insights. Simply speaking, we help the biggest companies solve some of their biggest problems, serving more than 5000 clients across the globe by providing research, data, and insights on their target markets. And, we’re proud to share we’ve received our Great Place to Work Certification for 2022! About Innovation: The Innovation Service Line guides clients throughout the innovation journey as they develop and launch new or improved products and services spanning a range of verticals. We help our clients develop the initial idea for a product/service, ensure the fully articulated description of the product/service resonates with consumers, confirm that prototypes of the product/service properly deliver on consumer expectations, and finetune the full mix prior to launch. This is accomplished using best-in-class services, including idea screening, concept testing, product testing, price and line optimization, package testing and volumetric forecasting. What you can expect to be doing: Quality control of consumer interviews and survey data Ensuring the quality and consistency of responses collected through our online surveys and face-to-face interviews conducted in our Retail Labs Reviewing responses to open-ended questions to identify inconsistent or unreliable interviews Monitoring survey quotas to ensure samples meet client expectations Conducting random checks on survey results to detect potential inconsistencies Reviewing videos collected during Mobile Eye Tracking studies to ensure the data is usable and of sufficient quality. Monitoring the execution of fieldwork protocols Ensuring that fieldwork protocols defined with our partners are properly implemented during face-to-face studies: Visiting our own facilities or partner locations when necessary to verify that all fieldwork materials and installations (shelves, survey equipment, etc.) are compliant with project requirements. Ensuring teams have been properly briefed and that interviews are conducted according to the agreed protocols. Training or retraining field teams on our protocols when necessary Monitoring and reporting on internal quality standards Supporting compliance with PRS IN VIVO internal quality processes: Ensuring Insights and Operations teams are familiar with PRS IN VIVO processes and able to apply them correctly. Reporting internal compliance status to global teams This might be the job for you if you have: Located within reasonable distance of the Parsippany, NJ office. Flexible and willing to travel to our various field locations. Highly organized and detail-oriented Able to work independently Proactive mindset Good command of Microsoft Office, particularly Excel If you don’t meet 100% of the requirements, we encourage all who feel they might be a fit for the opportunity to apply. We may consider a variety of backgrounds for a particular role and are also committed to considering candidates for available positions throughout our organization, not just the one you’re applying to! In accordance with NY/CO/CA/WA law, the estimated base salary range for this role is $48,000 to $52,000. Your final base salary will be determined based on several non-discriminatory factors which may include but are not limited to location, work experience, skills, knowledge, education and/or certifications. What’s in it for you: At Ipsos you’ll experience opportunities for Career Development, an exceptional benefits package (including generous PTO, healthcare plans, wellness benefits), a flexible workplace policy, and a strong collaborative culture. To find out more about all the great reasons to work at Ipsos, how we’re making an impact around the world, and more about our benefits and employee programs, please visit : Why Work at Ipsos | US Why Work at Ipsos | CA Inclusive Workplace Commitment Ipsos recognizes the necessity of building an inclusive culture that values each employee’s individuality and diverse perspectives. For more than 40 years, our mission has been to generate and analyze data about society, markets, brands, and behaviors to provide our clients with the insights that elevate their understanding of the world. This could not be fulfilled without Ipsos’ diverse employees who compile and analyze this data—they are the essence of who we are and what we do. We are committed to providing equal opportunity to all employees, creating an environment that promotes inclusion, and enabling employees from all walks of life to flourish. Ipsos encourages our employees to act in a respectful and responsible manner, in line with code of best practices concerning diversity and inclusion, human rights, equality, and civility for every individual. Ipsos is An Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, or any other protected class and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability. #LI-BM1 #LI-Remote
item.dc:creator: Ipsos
